assumptions: The developer has a supported browser and, for Agents SDK routes, a supported language runtime such as Python 3.10+ or Node.js 20+.
No LiveKit Cloud account, project, API keys, CLI link, or running agent exists yet.

developer_goal: Create a LiveKit Cloud account and reach the earliest official first voice or realtime agent success.

first_success.outcome: Unresolved between no-code Agent Builder, Voice AI SDK quickstart spoken response, and rooms SDK join flows.
first_success.observable_signal: Candidates include a browser Agent Builder session, an Agent Console spoken response from a local starter agent, or a rooms client joining a LiveKit room.

route_selection.surface: LiveKit Cloud with Agents quickstarts or realtime SDK docs
route_selection.rule: Prefer routes without payment or unavailable entitlement, then minimize actions; leave unresolved when Cloud signup is opaque and multiple official first-success surfaces remain.

candidates.name: LiveKit Agent Builder in the browser
candidates.reason: Official Voice AI docs present Agent Builder as a no-code browser path that may be shorter than CLI starter projects.

candidates.name: Voice AI quickstart with Python or Node starter via LiveKit CLI
candidates.reason: The Voice AI quickstart assumes a free Cloud account, CLI auth, starter init, and a spoken agent response, with Python and Node variants.

candidates.name: Self-hosted open-source LiveKit server
candidates.reason: The Cloud quickstart can be adapted for self-hosting, but that is a different operations surface from LiveKit Cloud signup.

required_path.action: Create a LiveKit Cloud account and free project from the Cloud login or signup entry.

required_fields.label: Cloud signup or identity-provider fields
required_fields.notes: Docs assume a free Cloud account and project; the login page does not publicly enumerate registration continuation states.

required_path.observable_result: The developer has a LiveKit Cloud project with API credentials available.

required_path.action: Complete the unresolved Agent Builder or CLI starter route through first spoken or realtime success.

required_fields.label: Route-specific CLI, starter, or Builder steps

required_path.observable_result: Depends on the unresolved LiveKit first-success route.

prerequisites.description: The Voice AI quickstart assumes a free LiveKit Cloud account with a project and API keys.
CLI-linked Cloud projects require installing the LiveKit CLI and running cloud auth for starter flows.

platform_outcomes.description: LiveKit Cloud provides hosted agent deployment, model inference, and realtime media transport on the free project path described in the quickstart.

excluded.item: Production agent deploy with lk agent create and custom frontends
excluded.reason: Deployment and custom clients occur after the local or Builder first-success demonstration.

excluded.item: Reading LiveKit conceptual docs before choosing a route
excluded.reason: Documentation reading is not a counted developer action.

sources.title: LiveKit Cloud sign in
evidence_supported: Cloud login entry; registration continuation states not fully public

sources.title: LiveKit CLI project commands
evidence_supported: CLI project linking and credential management

uncertainties.question: Which LiveKit first success is canonical: Agent Builder, Voice AI CLI starter spoken response, or a rooms SDK join?
uncertainties.impact: Required actions, runtimes, and whether Cloud CLI auth is mandatory all change.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: A workshop-owner route choice plus a fresh Cloud account walkthrough of that exact official path.

uncertainties.question: What exact sign-in, email-link, identity-provider, terms, and first-project creation states appear between cloud.livekit.io/login and a ready free project?
uncertainties.impact: Account creation is currently one opaque step, so field and wait counts cannot be verified.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Dated screenshots of LiveKit Cloud registration from empty state through first project credentials.
